channel.title: CodeSprintPro Tech Blog
channel.description: Practical backend engineering, system design, Java, databases, AWS, messaging, and AI infrastructure guides.

item.title: AI Infrastructure on AWS: SageMaker, EKS GPU Scheduling, and Cost-Efficient Inference
item.description: Production AI/ML infrastructure on AWS: SageMaker real-time vs async inference endpoints, EKS GPU scheduling with nvidia device plugin, EC2 GPU instance selection (p4, g5, inf2 Inferentia), Spot instances for training workloads, and the architecture decisions that keep GPU bills under control.

item.title: Kubernetes for AI Inference: GPUs, Autoscaling, Queues, and Cost Control
item.description: A practical guide to running AI inference on Kubernetes: GPU scheduling, node pools, taints and tolerations, model servers, queue-based autoscaling with KEDA, admission controls, observability, and cost guardrails.

item.title: LLM Evaluation at Scale: LLM-as-Judge, RAGAS, and Building Automated Eval Pipelines
item.description: How to evaluate LLM systems in production: LLM-as-judge patterns with bias mitigation, RAGAS metrics for RAG pipelines (faithfulness, context recall, answer relevancy), BERTScore vs ROUGE trade-offs, building regression test suites for prompts, and the statistical rigor needed to trust eval results.

item.title: LLM Inference Optimization: Quantization, KV Cache, and High-Throughput Serving
item.description: Production LLM serving: how quantization (GGUF, GPTQ, AWQ) cuts memory by 4x, KV cache memory math, continuous batching in vLLM, TensorRT-LLM for NVIDIA GPUs, and the throughput vs latency trade-offs that determine your serving architecture.

item.title: LLM Observability in Production: Traces, Evals, Cost, Latency, and Failure Modes
item.description: A production guide to LLM observability: OpenTelemetry traces, token and cost metrics, RAG retrieval spans, eval results, safety signals, prompt/version tracking, dashboards, alerts, and redaction patterns.

item.title: MCP for Backend Engineers: Tools, Agents, and Production Guardrails
item.description: A practical backend engineering guide to the Model Context Protocol: host-client-server architecture, tools vs resources vs prompts, JSON-RPC flows, authorization, audit logs, rate limits, idempotency, and production guardrails for AI agents.

item.title: PostgreSQL Locking Playbook: Deadlocks, Blocking Queries, and Timeouts
item.description: A practical PostgreSQL locking guide for production incidents: how to find blocking queries, understand lock modes, debug deadlocks, use lock_timeout and statement_timeout, avoid idle-in-transaction sessions, and design safer migrations.

item.title: Retrieval Systems for RAG: BM25, Dense Retrieval, Hybrid Search, and Reranking
item.description: How modern retrieval pipelines work: BM25 internals (TF-IDF, k1/b parameters), bi-encoder dense retrieval, why hybrid search beats both individually, Reciprocal Rank Fusion, cross-encoder reranking with ColBERT, HyDE query expansion, chunking strategies, and evaluating retrieval quality with NDCG and Recall@K.

item.title: Spring Boot Timeouts: The Production Guide for HTTP, DB, Redis, and Kafka
item.description: A practical Spring Boot timeout guide covering server shutdown, RestClient/WebClient, database pools, PostgreSQL statement timeouts, Redis, Kafka producers/consumers, retries, circuit breakers, and production defaults.

item.title: System Design: Building a Feature Flag Platform
item.description: Design a production feature flag platform: flag schemas, targeting rules, percentage rollouts, local SDK evaluation, streaming updates, audit logs, kill switches, experiments, consistency tradeoffs, and flag lifecycle management.

item.title: System Design: Building a Distributed Job Scheduler
item.description: Design a production job scheduler like Quartz, Airflow, or Cloud Scheduler: cron parsing, durable job storage, leases, retries, idempotency, delayed execution, worker pools, multi-tenancy, observability, and failure recovery.

item.title: System Design: Building a Metrics Platform Like Prometheus
item.description: Design a production metrics platform: ingestion, scraping vs push, time-series storage, labels and cardinality, rollups, retention, alerting, query APIs, multi-tenancy, downsampling, and operational guardrails.

item.title: System Design: Building a Webhook Delivery Platform
item.description: Design a production webhook delivery platform with event ingestion, outbox persistence, retries, exponential backoff, signing, endpoint secrets, idempotency, rate limits, dead-letter queues, replay, observability, and tenant isolation.

item.title: Idempotency Keys in APIs: Retries, Duplicate Requests, and Exactly-Once Illusions
item.description: A production guide to designing idempotent APIs with idempotency keys, request fingerprinting, response replay, database constraints, TTL cleanup, and race-condition handling.

item.title: Cache Invalidation Patterns: TTL, Write-Through, Cache-Aside, and Event-Driven Eviction
item.description: A practical guide to cache invalidation in production systems: TTLs, cache-aside, write-through, versioned keys, event-driven eviction, stampede prevention, and stale-read tradeoffs.

item.title: Database Connection Pool Tuning: HikariCP, PostgreSQL, and Traffic Spikes
item.description: A practical guide to tuning database connection pools for Spring Boot services: pool sizing, HikariCP settings, PostgreSQL limits, slow query impact, metrics, and production failure modes.

item.title: Kafka Consumer Lag: Causes, Diagnosis, and Production Fixes
item.description: A practical Kafka consumer lag playbook covering partition skew, slow processing, rebalances, max.poll settings, poison messages, autoscaling, metrics, and safe recovery strategies.

item.title: Multi-Region Architecture: Active-Active, Active-Passive, and Consistency Trade-Offs
item.description: A practical guide to multi-region system design: active-active vs active-passive, DNS failover, RPO/RTO, data replication, conflict resolution, global databases, and when not to go multi-region.

item.title: Production Incident Playbooks: Debugging Latency, Errors, and Traffic Spikes
item.description: A practical incident response playbook for backend engineers: how to triage production latency, error-rate spikes, database issues, queue backlogs, and rollback decisions without guessing under pressure.

item.title: Spring Boot Production Readiness Checklist: Timeouts, Pools, Health Checks, and Observability
item.description: A practical Spring Boot production checklist covering HTTP timeouts, HikariCP, thread pools, Actuator health checks, graceful shutdown, structured logging, metrics, resilience, and deployment safety.

item.title: System Design: Building an Audit Log System for Compliance and Debugging
item.description: Design a production audit log system with immutable events, schema design, write paths, search, retention, tamper resistance, PII controls, partitioning, and compliance tradeoffs.

item.title: System Design: Building a Feature Store for Real-Time Machine Learning
item.description: Design a practical feature store for production ML systems: offline and online features, point-in-time correctness, streaming updates, Redis/DynamoDB serving, monitoring, and training-serving skew.

item.title: Transactional Outbox Pattern: Reliable Event Publishing Without Dual Writes
item.description: A production guide to the transactional outbox pattern: schema design, polling publishers, Debezium CDC, Kafka publishing, retries, ordering, cleanup, and exactly-once myths.

item.title: Using Open Source Models with Claude Code: A Developer's Guide
item.description: Learn how to leverage open source AI models with Claude Code CLI for enhanced productivity, from local inference to custom agents. Perfect for developers who value privacy and customization.

item.title: Java Concurrency Patterns: CompletableFuture, Structured Concurrency, and Thread-Safe Design
item.description: Production Java concurrency: CompletableFuture pipelines, handling exceptions in async chains, Java 21 structured concurrency, thread-safe collection patterns, and the concurrency bugs that cause data corruption.

item.title: Building Production Observability with OpenTelemetry and Grafana Stack
item.description: End-to-end observability implementation: distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry, metrics with Prometheus, structured logging with Loki, and the dashboards and alerts that actually help during incidents.

item.title: AWS Lambda in Production: Cold Starts, Concurrency, and Cost Optimization
item.description: How Lambda execution environments work, cold start mitigation strategies, concurrency limits and throttling, Lambda power tuning, VPC networking costs, and when Lambda is the wrong tool.

item.title: Event Sourcing and CQRS in Production: Beyond the Theory
item.description: What event sourcing actually looks like in production Java systems: event store design, snapshot strategies, projection rebuilding, CQRS read model synchronization, and the operational challenges nobody talks about.

item.title: Cassandra Data Modeling: Design for Queries, Not Entities
item.description: Apache Cassandra data modeling from first principles: partition key design, clustering columns, denormalization strategies, avoiding hot partitions, materialized views vs. manual duplication, and the anti-patterns that kill Cassandra performance.

item.title: gRPC vs REST vs GraphQL: Choosing the Right API Protocol
item.description: A technical comparison of REST, gRPC, and GraphQL across performance, developer experience, schema evolution, streaming, and real production use cases. When each protocol wins and where each falls short.

item.title: DynamoDB Advanced Patterns: Single-Table Design and Beyond
item.description: Production DynamoDB: single-table design with access pattern mapping, GSI overloading, sparse indexes, adjacency lists for graph relationships, DynamoDB Streams for event-driven architectures, and the read/write capacity math that prevents bill shock.

item.title: Java Memory Management Deep Dive: Heap, GC, and Production Tuning
item.description: How the JVM allocates memory, how G1GC and ZGC work under the hood, heap analysis with JVM tools, and the GC tuning decisions that eliminate latency spikes in production Java services.

item.title: Kubernetes in Production: Patterns Every Backend Engineer Must Know
item.description: Resource requests and limits, liveness vs readiness probes, rolling deployments, HPA configuration, pod disruption budgets, and the mistakes that cause production outages in Kubernetes.

item.title: Zero-Downtime Database Migrations: Patterns for Production
item.description: How to safely migrate production databases without downtime: expand-contract pattern, backward-compatible schema changes, rolling deployments with dual-write, column renaming strategies, and the PostgreSQL-specific techniques for large table alterations.

item.title: PostgreSQL Performance Tuning: From Slow Queries to Sub-Millisecond Reads
item.description: A production guide to PostgreSQL query optimization: EXPLAIN ANALYZE, index design, VACUUM tuning, connection pooling with PgBouncer, partitioning, and the configuration changes that actually move the needle.

item.title: Spring Security OAuth2 and JWT: Production Implementation Guide
item.description: Complete Spring Security OAuth2 implementation: JWT token validation, Resource Server configuration, method-level security, custom UserDetailsService, refresh token rotation, and the security pitfalls that lead to authentication bypasses.

item.title: Elasticsearch Query Optimization: From Slow to Sub-100ms
item.description: Elasticsearch performance tuning in production: query vs filter context, mapping optimization, shard sizing strategy, field data vs doc values, aggregation performance, index lifecycle management, and the profiling tools that identify bottlenecks.

item.title: Scaling Spring Boot Applications to Handle 10 Million Daily Active Users
item.description: A practical performance engineering guide: load balancing, horizontal scaling, database tuning, JVM optimization, autoscaling, and the observability stack to find and fix bottlenecks before they page you.

item.title: Multi-Tenancy Architecture: Database, Application, and Infrastructure Patterns
item.description: Production multi-tenancy: database isolation models (shared schema, shared database, separate database), tenant routing, data partitioning strategies, cross-tenant query prevention, Spring Boot tenant context propagation, and the trade-offs at each isolation level.

item.title: Designing a Retry System Without Causing a Retry Storm
item.description: Exponential backoff with jitter, circuit breakers, bulkhead isolation, Kafka retry topics, and the retry amplification problem — with Java implementations and a real outage postmortem.

item.title: Feature Flags and Progressive Delivery: Safe Releases at Scale
item.description: Production feature flag implementation: flag evaluation architecture, percentage rollouts, user targeting, kill switches, flag lifecycle management, OpenFeature SDK, LaunchDarkly patterns, and how progressive delivery eliminates release fear.

item.title: Microservices Are Overrated for Most Startups
item.description: A contrarian but technically grounded case for starting with a well-structured monolith. Distributed transaction costs, network latency math, observability overhead, and when to actually break services apart.

item.title: Terraform Infrastructure as Code: Production Patterns and Pitfalls
item.description: Production Terraform: module design, state management with S3 and DynamoDB locking, workspace strategies for multi-environment deployments, sensitive variable handling, drift detection, and the Terraform anti-patterns that cause outages.

item.title: Designing a High-Throughput Notification System for 100K Events per Second
item.description: End-to-end architecture for a notification system handling 100,000 events per second: capacity planning, Kafka partition sizing, fan-out strategy, rate limiting, idempotency, and incident simulation.

item.title: Java Profiling and Heap Analysis: Finding Memory Leaks and CPU Bottlenecks
item.description: Production Java profiling: async-profiler for CPU and allocation profiling, heap dump analysis with Eclipse MAT, finding memory leaks, GC log analysis, detecting thread contention, JVM flags for profiling in production, and reading flame graphs.

item.title: WebSocket and SSE for Real-Time Systems: Architecture and Production Patterns
item.description: Building real-time features at scale: WebSocket vs SSE trade-offs, Spring Boot WebSocket implementation with STOMP, connection management, horizontal scaling with Redis pub/sub, SSE for one-directional streaming, and handling reconnection and backpressure.

item.title: Redis Caching Strategy at Scale: Beyond Simple Key-Value
item.description: Cache stampede, penetration, avalanche, eviction policy selection, clustering, and persistence trade-offs for production Redis deployments. With Java examples and a real production incident walkthrough.

item.title: Cloud Cost Optimization: Engineering Practices That Cut AWS Bills by 50%
item.description: Systematic AWS cost reduction: right-sizing EC2 and RDS instances, Savings Plans vs Reserved Instances, S3 lifecycle policies, data transfer cost elimination, EKS node optimization, RDS read replicas vs caching, and the observability stack for cost monitoring.

item.title: Designing Idempotent Payment Systems in Distributed Architecture
item.description: How duplicate payments happen in real systems, idempotency key design, race condition handling, Redis vs DB for idempotency stores, and the production incident that shaped our architecture.

item.title: Kafka Streams: Real-Time Stream Processing Without a Separate Cluster
item.description: Production Kafka Streams: KStream vs KTable semantics, stateful transformations with RocksDB state stores, windowed aggregations, stream-table joins, topology design, changelog topics, and the operational patterns for running Kafka Streams in production.
